title: Meet the Sahaba: Abu Bakr as-Siddiq, the Prophet's Closest Friend

sound.ambient: calm desert night ambience, soft warm wind, distant and serene, vast and clean, no music, no melody, no instruments

_note: Meet the Sahaba #1 - Abu Bakr as-Siddiq. Aniconic (no faces/figures) warm parchment/gold sahaba-* kit + sand theme. Quran 9:40 (the cave) auto-pulled. Hadith (Sahih al-Bukhari, the khalil hadith) verified + on fact sheet. Biographical facts are widely-reported history, attributed not as hadith. Shop-waitlist ad closer. No em dashes.

segments.text: There is one man the Prophet, peace be upon him, called his brother and companion, the one he would have chosen as his closest friend. This is the story of that man.
segments.caption: Abu Bakr as-Siddiq.

segments.text: Abu Bakr as-Siddiq. The Truthful. The closest companion of the Messenger of Allah.
Among the first to believe. The one who freed Bilal from slavery. And the only man chosen to hide beside the Prophet.
The Quran records that moment. When they were both in the cave, and the Prophet said to him: do not grieve, indeed Allah is with us.

segments.arabic: لَوْ كُنْتُ مُتَّخِذًا مِنْ أُمَّتِي خَلِيلًا لَاتَّخَذْتُ أَبَا بَكْرٍ، وَلَكِنْ أَخِي وَصَاحِبِي
segments.translation: If I were to take a Khalil, I would have taken Abu Bakr, but he is my brother and my companion.
segments.text: And the Prophet himself said: if I were to take a khalil, the most beloved of friends, I would have taken Abu Bakr; but he is my brother and my companion.

data.heading: His journey
rows: First among men to answer the call
Hid with the Prophet during the Hijrah
Helped build the first Muslim community
Chosen as the first Caliph of Islam

segments.text: From the streets of Mecca, to the cave of Thawr, to Medina, and finally to leading the Muslims after the Prophet was gone.
When others doubted the Night Journey to Jerusalem and the heavens, he simply said: if he said it, then it is true. And so he became as-Siddiq.
segments.caption: The Truthful.

data.heading: His legacy
items: He gave his entire wealth for Islam.
He freed Bilal and other enslaved believers.
He never wavered when others did.
He led the Muslims as the first Caliph.

segments.text: He gave everything he owned. He freed the enslaved. He stood firm when others wavered. And he led with humility.
The closest friend. The first Caliph. The Truthful. This was Abu Bakr as-Siddiq, may Allah be pleased with him.
segments.caption: Radiya Allahu anhu.

segments.text: And comment the companion you want me to feature next.
segments.caption: Who next?

data.headline: Something beautiful is coming
data.sub: Personalized storybooks, photo keepsakes, and cards sealed with a dua.

segments.text: And something beautiful is coming from Ketabi Studio. Personalized storybooks, keepsakes, and cards, each one sealed with a prayer. Join the founding list now, at Ketabi Studio dot com.

sources: Abu Bakr as-Siddiq (RA) - first caliph of Islam; companion of the Prophet in the cave during the Hijrah
Quran 9:40 (at-Tawbah) - 'the second of two, when they were in the cave' - auto-pulled
Sahih al-Bukhari 3656 - 'Were I to take a khalil other than my Lord, I would have taken Abu Bakr' (verified hadith)
Title as-Siddiq for affirming the Night Journey (Isra and Mi'raj); freed Bilal; gave his wealth - widely-reported sira/history

claims.claim: The Prophet said: 'If I were to take a Khalil (most beloved friend), I would have taken Abu Bakr, but he is my brother and my companion'
The Quran places Abu Bakr with the Prophet in the cave: 'the second of two... do not grieve, Allah is with us'
He was titled as-Siddiq (the Truthful), associated with affirming the Night Journey (Isra)
He freed Bilal ibn Rabah and other enslaved believers
He became the first Caliph of Islam after the Prophet (632 CE)
